items.question_text: Complete the prescribed GPS/WAAS outage advisory: "ATTENTION ALL AIRCRAFT GPS REPORTED UNRELIABLE OR WAAS UNAVAILABLE IN VICINITY/AREA _____"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-10, the approved advisory ends with "POSITION."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed USAF weather-avoidance coordination phraseology: "PROCEDURES ARE APPLIED AT BOTH _____ BY THE USAF FACILITY"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-12, the approved phraseology uses "LOCATIONS."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed formation join-up instruction: "ROOK01 CLIMB AND _____ FLIGHT LEVEL TWO ZERO ZERO REPORT ADVISE WHEN FORMATION JOIN-UP IS COMPLETE"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-13, the approved instruction is "CLIMB AND MAINTAIN FLIGHT LEVEL TWO ZERO ZERO..."

items.question_text: If your control instruction will be relayed to the pilot by a source other than another radar controller, who remains responsible for ensuring required coordination is complete?
items.explanation: Per 2-1-14, the controller issuing the instruction remains responsible for ensuring all required coordination is completed.

choices.text: The person relaying the message, because they are the last link to the pilot.
The pilot, because accepting the instruction implies coordination authority.
The receiving controller only after the aircraft crosses the boundary.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed frequency-change instruction: "UNITED TWO TWENTY-TWO CHANGE TO MY FREQUENCY ONE TWO _____ POINT FOUR"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-17, the approved example uses "ONE TWO THREE POINT FOUR."

items.question_text: Before clearing an aircraft through a surface area controlled by a nonapproach control tower, what is required?
items.explanation: Per 2-1-16, coordination with the appropriate nonapproach control tower is required on an individual-aircraft basis unless an LOA says otherwise.

choices.text: Coordinate with the appropriate tower unless an LOA or other authorized procedure already covers it.
Tell the pilot to obtain tower authorization directly before entering the surface area.
Wait until the aircraft is inside the surface area, then advise the tower.
No coordination is required as long as the aircraft is receiving radar service.

items.question_text: When an aircraft is already in contact with a radar facility, who is expected to obtain authorization through each surface area the aircraft will transit?
items.explanation: Per 2-1-16, the pilot is not expected to obtain separate authorization through each area while in contact with a radar facility.

choices.text: The radar facility handling the aircraft, not the pilot.
The pilot, by contacting each tower independently before entry.
The airport operator for each affected surface area.
No one, because entry into each surface area is automatic once radar contact exists.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed advisory when radar vectors cannot be provided: "NOVEMBER 123 _____ RADAR VECTORS YOU ARE NOT UNDER MY JURISDICTION"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-21, the approved advisory uses "UNABLE."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed report request: "AIRCRAFT PASSES THE OUTER MARKER/FINAL _____ FIX"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-25, the approved report request is "FINAL APPROACH FIX."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed pilot-deviation contact instruction: "IDENTIFICATION POSSIBLE PILOT DEVIATION ADVISE YOU _____ FACILITY AT TELEPHONE NUMBER"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-27, the approved instruction uses "CONTACT."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed RVSM point-out phraseology: "POINT OUT BAXTER1 CLIMBING TO FL 360 _____ RVSM"
items.explanation: Per 2-1-29, the approved phraseology uses "NEGATIVE RVSM."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed amended-flight-data transmission: "AMERICAN TWO REVISED _____ LEVEL THREE THREE ZERO"
items.explanation: Per 2-2-11, the approved transmission is "REVISED FLIGHT LEVEL THREE THREE ZERO."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed equipment-suffix example: "N12345 _____ AAL192 LN751B"
items.explanation: Per 2-3-5, the approved example includes the suffix "TN5552Q."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed accident/incident phraseology: "_____ PERTAINING TO THE INCIDENT"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-6, the approved wording uses "INFORMATION."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed interphone example: "MIAMI _____ JETSTAR ONE THIS IS SAFEAIR ONE MESSAGE"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-7, the approved interphone example uses "CENTER."

items.question_text: Which sequence matches the standard format for initial radio contact with an aircraft?
items.explanation: Per 2-4-8, the initial format is aircraft identification, ATC unit identification, the message if any, and "over" if required.

choices.text: Aircraft identification, ATC unit identification, message if any, and "over" if required.
ATC unit identification, aircraft identification, message if any, and "over" if required.
Message first, then aircraft identification, ATC unit identification, and "over" if required.
Aircraft identification, message if any, ATC unit identification, and "over" if required.

items.question_text: After communications are established with a U.S. civil aircraft using its registration number, how may the identification be abbreviated?
items.explanation: Per 2-4-9, use the identification prefix and the last three digits or letters.

choices.text: Use the identification prefix and the last three digits or letters.
Use only the last two digits or letters once the pilot has replied.
Drop the prefix and use any convenient shortened version.
Use the full registration until the aircraft has landed.

items.question_text: Which identification is one example that must not be abbreviated?
items.explanation: Per 2-4-9, similar-sounding identifications may not be abbreviated.

choices.text: A U.S. civil registration after communications are established.
A call sign that the pilot has already read back correctly once.
A registration number that ends in only three letters.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed receiver approval example: "RECEIVER AMERICAN TWENTY-FIVE FLIGHT LEVEL THREE FIVE ZERO _____ AC"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-12, the approved transmission ends with "APPROVED AC."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed duplicate-call-sign instruction: "CALL SIGNS WHEN THE AIRCRAFT ARE OPERATING _____ WITHIN THE SAME SECTOR"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-15, the approved wording uses "SIMULTANEOUSLY."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altimeter-setting example: "SETTING STATEMENT 3001 _____ THREE ZERO ZERO ONE"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-17, the approved wording is "3001 ALTIMETER THREE ZERO ZERO ONE."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed facility-identification example: "COLUMBUS _____ BARKSDALE TOWER NAVY JACKSONVILLE TOWER"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-19, the approved identification is "COLUMBUS TOWER."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed aircraft-type description example: "L-TEN-ELEVEN AMERICAN MD-EIGHTY SEVEN THIRTY-SEVEN BOEING _____ FIFTY-SEVEN"
items.explanation: Per 2-4-21, the approved aircraft description is "BOEING SEVEN FIFTY-SEVEN."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed route-designator example: "NORTH AMERICAN ROUTE SIXTY-SEVEN _____ BAHAMA ROUTE FIFTY-FIVE VICTOR"
items.explanation: Per 2-5-1, the approved route-designator example uses "BRAVO."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed weather-deviation instruction: "UNABLE REQUESTED DEVIATION FLY HEADING HEADING _____ CLEAR OF WEATHER"
items.explanation: Per 2-6-4, the approved instruction is "ADVISE CLEAR OF WEATHER."

items.question_text: How should wind be described when the velocity is less than three knots?
items.explanation: Per 2-6-3, wind below three knots is described as calm.

choices.text: As light and variable only if it is below one knot.
As omitted from the report unless the pilot asks.

items.question_text: If prevailing visibility is below 4 miles and the tower and surface observations differ, which value is used for aircraft operations?
items.explanation: Per 2-6-3, the lower of the two observations is used for aircraft operations.

choices.text: The lower of the two observations.
The average of the tower and surface observations.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altimeter-advisory example: "NEAREST REPORTING STATION ALONG THE AIRCRAFT'S ROUTE OF _____"
items.explanation: Per 2-7-2, the approved advisory uses "FLIGHT."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed altimeter-advisory example: "1500 FEET AGL _____ IS LOWER"
items.explanation: Per 2-7-3, the approved advisory uses "WHICHEVER IS LOWER."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed RVR phraseology: "_____ ONE FOUR RVR TWO THOUSAND FOUR HUNDRED"
items.explanation: Per 2-8-3, the approved RVR example begins with "RUNWAY."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed ATIS diversion advisory: "AT ONE-TWO-FIVE-ZERO ZULU ADVISE ON INITIAL _____ IF YOU WANT TO DIVERT"
items.explanation: Per 2-9-3, the approved advisory is "ADVISE ON INITIAL CONTACT IF YOU WANT TO DIVERT."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed runway-crossing instruction: "_____ RUNWAY AT POINT/INTERSECTION"
items.explanation: Per 3-1-3, the approved instruction begins with "CROSS."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed wind-shear statement: "_____ DEPARTURE/THRESHOLD WIND DIRECTION AT VELOCITY"
items.explanation: Per 3-1-8, the approved statement begins with "RUNWAY."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed uncertified-display advisory: "IDENTIFICATION SUGGESTED _____ DEGREES"
items.explanation: Per 3-1-9, the approved advisory uses "HEADING."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed abnormal-condition advisory: "ITEM APPEAR/S _____ CONDITION"
items.explanation: Per 3-1-10, the approved advisory uses "OBSERVED CONDITION."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed taxi instruction: "IDENTIFICATION _____ TO VIA ROUTE"
items.explanation: Per 3-1-15, the approved taxi instruction uses "TAXI."

items.question_text: If a pilot-in-command requests to return to the ramp, gate, or alternate deplaning area under the Three/Four-Hour Tarmac Rule, what should tower do?
items.explanation: Per 3-1-15, the request should be accommodated as soon as operationally practical unless it would cause a significant operational disruption.

choices.text: Provide the requested return as soon as operationally practical, or advise that it cannot be accommodated because it would significantly disrupt operations.
Deny the request automatically until the aircraft reaches the front of the departure sequence.
Grant the request only if the airport operator suspends all other surface movements first.
Tell the pilot to remain in place until the airline coordinates directly with the tower.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed arresting-system advisory: "_____ ONE FOUR ARRESTING CABLE ONE THOUSAND FEET FROM THRESHOLD"
items.explanation: Per 3-3-6, the approved advisory begins with "RUNWAY."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed localizer-monitor advisory: "_____ MONITOR INDICATES RUNWAY NUMBER LOCALIZER UNRELIABLE"
items.explanation: Per 3-3-7, the approved advisory begins with "CAUTION."

items.question_text: Which is one approved way to identify an observed target or track on an ASDE display?
items.explanation: Per 3-6-2, one approved method is to correlate the target with a pilot or vehicle operator position report.

choices.text: Correlate it with a pilot or vehicle operator position report.
Assume the nearest moving target is the expected aircraft if its speed looks plausible.
Identify it solely by airport-map location without any report or observation.
Treat any moving target as identified once it stays on the display for 30 seconds.

items.question_text: If the area containing a suspected false target is not visible from the tower, what may be used to conduct the visual observation?
items.explanation: Per 3-6-2, an airport operations vehicle or pilots of aircraft operating in the area may be used to conduct the visual observation.

choices.text: An airport operations vehicle or pilots of aircraft operating in the area.
Only airport maintenance personnel working outside the movement area.
Only the approach control radar-associate position.
No one; the target must remain displayed until tower can see it directly.

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed ground-movement instruction: "_____ LEFT/RIGHT ON THE TAXIWAY/RUNWAY YOU ARE APPROACHING"
items.explanation: Per 3-6-3, the approved instruction begins with "TURN."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed intersection-departure advisory: "RUNWAY NUMBER AT TAXIWAY DESIGNATOR INTERSECTION DEPARTURE REMAINING LENGTH FEET _____"
items.explanation: Per 3-7-1, the approved advisory ends with "AVAILABLE."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed taxi instruction across an active runway: "RUNWAY THREE-SIX LEFT TAXI VIA ALPHA CHARLIE _____ RUNWAY ONE-ZERO"
items.explanation: Per 3-7-2, the approved instruction uses "CROSS RUNWAY ONE-ZERO."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed wake/rotor-wash caution: "FOLLOW CH-53 RUNWAY TWO-ONE TAXI VIA BRAVO _____ ROTOR WASH"
items.explanation: Per 3-7-3, the approved caution is "CAUTION ROTOR WASH."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed ILS critical-area instruction: "_____ SHORT OF RUNWAY ILS CRITICAL AREA"
items.explanation: Per 3-7-5, the approved instruction begins with "HOLD."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed missed-approach vehicle advisory: "DELTA 1058 IN THE EVENT OF MISSED APPROACH VEHICLE LEFT OF _____"
items.explanation: Per 3-7-6, the approved advisory ends with "RUNWAY."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed traffic-position instruction: "CLEARED FOR TAKEOFF OR _____ SHORT/HOLD IN POSITION/TAXI OFF THE RUNWAY TRAFFIC"
items.explanation: Per 3-8-1, the approved traffic instruction uses "HOLD."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed opposite-direction traffic advisory: "TRAFFIC DESCRIPTION ARRIVING/DEPARTING/LOW _____ OPPOSITE DIRECTION ON PARALLEL RUNWAY/LANDING STRIP"
items.explanation: Per 3-8-4, the approved advisory uses "LOW APPROACH."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed line-up-and-wait instruction: "AMERICAN 528 _____ TWO-THREE CONTINUE TRAFFIC HOLDING IN POSITION"
items.explanation: Per 3-9-4, the approved instruction uses "RUNWAY TWO-THREE CONTINUE..."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed wake-turbulence instruction: "_____ FOR WAKE TURBULENCE"
items.explanation: Per 3-9-6, the approved instruction is "HOLD FOR WAKE TURBULENCE."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed wake-turbulence instruction: "_____ FOR WAKE TURBULENCE"
items.explanation: Per 3-9-7, the approved instruction is "HOLD FOR WAKE TURBULENCE."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed shortened-runway takeoff clearance: "RUNWAY NUMBER AT TAXIWAY DESIGNATOR INTERSECTION DEPARTURE SHORTENED _____ FOR TAKEOFF"
items.explanation: Per 3-9-10, the approved phraseology is "SHORTENED CLEARED FOR TAKEOFF."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed landing clearance: "RUNWAY NUMBER _____ TO LAND"
items.explanation: Per 3-10-5, the approved clearance is "RUNWAY NUMBER CLEARED TO LAND."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed runway-crossing/hold-short instruction after landing: "AMERICAN FOUR NINETY-TWO TURN LEFT AT TAXIWAY CHARLIE _____ SHORT OF RUNWAY 27 RIGHT"
items.explanation: Per 3-10-9, the approved instruction uses "HOLD SHORT OF RUNWAY 27 RIGHT."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed low-approach clearance: "_____ LOW APPROACH AT OR ABOVE ALTITUDE TRAFFIC DESCRIPTION AND LOCATION"
items.explanation: Per 3-10-10, the approved phraseology begins with "CLEARED."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed closed-traffic approval: "LEFT/RIGHT IF REQUIRED CLOSED TRAFFIC _____ REPORT POSITION IF REQUIRED"
items.explanation: Per 3-10-11, the approved phraseology uses "APPROVED."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed overhead-maneuver instruction: "AIR FORCE THREE SIX EIGHT BREAK AT MIDFIELD _____ BREAK"
items.explanation: Per 3-10-12, the approved instruction is "BREAK AT MIDFIELD REPORT BREAK."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed takeoff-cancellation instruction: "_____ FOR REASON- TAKEOFF CLEARANCE RELEASE LANDING/TAXIING AIRCRAFT ETC"
items.explanation: Per 3-11-1, the approved instruction begins with "HOLD."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed departure-turn instruction: "MAKE RIGHT/LEFT _____ FOR DEPARTURE/DEPARTURE ROUTE AVOID AIRCRAFT/VEHICLES/PERSONNEL"
items.explanation: Per 3-11-2, the approved instruction uses "TURN."

items.question_text: Complete the prescribed arrival-turn instruction: "MAKE APPROACH STRAIGHT-IN/CIRCLING LEFT/RIGHT _____ TO ARRIVAL/ARRIVAL ROUTE"
items.explanation: Per 3-11-6, the approved instruction uses "TURN."
